title: Congo's Ebola outbreak tops 2,000 cases, kills 754 in fastest-growing surge
summary_full: In the Democratic Republic of Congo, the Ebola situation has worsened, now exceeding two thousand confirmed infections and recording 754 lives lost. This outbreak has become the most rapid on record, overwhelming health authorities. Reports of suspected cases in additional provinces suggest wider transmission, though new treatment trials targeting the Bundibugyo strain provide potential avenues for relief amid severe challenges in containment.
